column1: Login page accepts passwords shorter than the required minimum length of 8 characters
Enter a valid username or email address.
Enter a password containing only 3 characters (e.g., abc).
The application should reject passwords shorter than 8 characters and display a validation message such as:
Password must be at least 8 characters long.
The login request should not be processed.
The application accepts a 3-character password and proceeds with the login process instead of enforcing the minimum password length requirement.

This is a functional and security-related issue because the application fails to enforce the specified password policy. Weak passwords increase the risk of unauthorized access. However, the application is still operational, so it is not considered Critical.

This defect should be fixed as soon as possible because it affects authentication security and violates the documented requirement. Password validation issues should be addressed before the application is released.

Verify password containing special characters/spaces only

column1: 3. Enter a password containing only special characters or spaces (e.g., !!!!!!!!, @@@@@@@@, or ).

column2: The system should validate according to the password policy. If passwords require at least one letter or number, the input should be rejected with an appropriate error message. If no such rule exists, the system should still correctly enforce the minimum length and reject passwords consisting only of spaces.
